data.title: Sector Update: Financial Stocks Decline Late Afternoon
Dai-Ichi Life Holdings, Inc. Announces Abolishment of Protection and Retirement, Savings and Asset Management Business Units
data.description: Dai-ichi Life Holdings, Inc. announced that as part of the reorganization, the "Protection Business Unit" will be abolished. Additionally, the company also announced the abolition of the "Retirement, Savings and Asset Management Business Unit".

data.title: Dai-Ichi Life Holdings, Inc. Announces Executive and Board Changes, Effective April 1, 2026
data.description:
Dai-ichi Life Holdings, Inc. announced the changes of officers and organizational restructuring effective April 1, 2026. Ryosuke Kondo, an external appointee most recently affiliated with Benesse Corporation, will become Executive Officer and concurrently Representative Director and President of Daiichi Neo Life Insurance Co. Ltd. effective the same date.

Kyoichi Haida, currently Managing Executive Officer at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, will serve as Managing Executive Officer (Status) and additionally become Director, Senior Executive Officer and Chief Compliance Officer of Daiichi Frontier Life Insurance Co. Ltd. Sanae Iguchi will be appointed Executive Officer (Status) and simultaneously assume the role of Representative Director and President of Daiichi Life Bussan Co. Ltd., while Tadashi Sobue becomes Executive Officer (Status) and then Director, Managing Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer of Daiichi Frontier Life Insurance Co.

Ltd. beginning April 1, 2026. Masashi Kataoka, who has been Head of Alternative Investment Unit, will now officially serve as Expert Executive Officer and Head of Alternative Investment Unit.Ryosuke Kondo graduated in March 2003 from Meiji University, School of Political Science and Economics, and began his career in April 2003 at The Pioneer Corporation. In January 2016, he became General Manager of the Corporate Planning Department at LIFENET INSURANCE COMPANY, followed by his appointment as General Manager of the Sales Planning Department in April 2017.

By June 2018, he had advanced to Executive Officer and General Manager of the Sales & Marketing Division, and in July 2019 he served as Executive Officer overseeing the Corporate Planning, Product Development, and Finance Strategy Departments. In June 2021, he became Director and Executive Officer responsible for Corporate Planning, Product Development, and Finance Strategy, and in January 2022 he continued as Director and Executive Officer of the Sales & Marketing Division. Beginning June 2022, he further expanded his responsibilities to include the Data Science Promotion Office while also serving as Director of LIFENET MIRAI Inc. In April 2024, he became Director and Executive Officer of the Partner Business Department as well as Director of LIFENET MIRAI Inc. His career progressed further in June 2024 when he was appointed Senior Executive Officer of the Partner Business Department and simultaneously served as Representative Director, President, and Chief Executive Officer of LIFENET MIRAI Inc. Most recently, in October 2025, he joined Benesse Corporation as Deputy General Manager of the Operation Transformation Division.

Kyoichi Haida graduated from Kwansei Gakuin University, Faculty of Letters, in March 1991 and joined The Dai-ichi Mutual Life Insurance Company the following month. In April 2012, he became Deputy General Manager of the Underwriting and Medical Department at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, and in April 2014 he advanced to General Manager of the same department. One year later, in April 2015, he was appointed General Manager of the Business Process Planning Department, and in October 2016 he continued in this role at the restructured The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited.

His responsibilities increased further in April 2019 when he became Executive Officer and General Manager of the Business Process Planning Department. He continued as an Executive Officer in April 2020 and was promoted to Director and Managing Executive Officer in April 2022, eventually reaching the role of Managing Executive Officer in April 2025. Sanae Iguchi graduated from Rikkyo University, College of Sociology, in March 1993 and joined The Dai-ichi Mutual Life Insurance Company in April 1993.

In April 2018, she became Deputy General Manager of the Total Life Planning and Sales Training Department at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, followed by her appointment as Deputy General Manager of the Human Resources Department in April 2019. Her progression continued in April 2020 when she became Line Manager of the Human Resources Department, and in April 2021 she advanced to General Manager of the same department. Most recently, in April 2024, she was appointed Executive Officer, further solidifying her leadership within the company.

Tadashi Sobue graduated from The University of Tokyo, Faculty of Science, in March 1998 and began his career in April 1998 at Boston Consulting Group K.K. (now Boston Consulting Group LLC). In April 2016, he became Head of the Financial Product Department at Goldman Sachs Japan Co. Ltd., and in June 2019 he returned to Boston Consulting Group K.K. as Partner & Director.

He later joined Aon Group Japan Ltd. in December 2021 before moving in June 2022 to Apollo Management Japan Limited as Managing Director and Head of Japan, concurrently serving as Japan Representative for Athene Life Re Ltd. (Bermuda) and Athene Annuity & Life Company (US). His leadership responsibilities expanded further in August 2022 when he became Director of Athene Employee Services, K.K. (now Athene Japan K.K.). In November 2022, he became Senior Vice President, and in January 2023 he was appointed Director and Senior Vice President of Athene Re Japan Solutions Co.

Ltd. After becoming Senior Advisor in September 2024, he joined The Dai-ichi Frontier Life Insurance Co. Ltd. as Executive Officer in September 2025.Masashi Kataoka graduated from Keio University, Faculty of Economics, in March 1997 and joined The Dai-ichi Mutual Life Insurance Company in April 1997. In April 2018, he became Deputy General Manager of the Equity Investment Department at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, and in April 2019 he transitioned to Deputy General Manager of the Alternative Investment Department.

His expertise in investment strategy led to his appointment as General Manager of the Alternative Investment Department in April 2022. Most recently, in October 2025, he assumed the role of Head of the Alternative Investment Unit at Dai-ichi Life Holdings, Inc., continuing his long-standing contributions to the company?s investment leadership.

data.title: Dai-Ichi Life Holdings, Inc. Announces Executive and Board Changes, Effective April 1, 2026
data.description:
Dai-ichi Life Holdings, Inc. announced the changes of officers and organizational restructuring effective April 1, 2026. Hitoshi Yamaguchi will assume the role of Representative Director, Senior Managing Executive Officer and Group Chief Human Resources Officer, transitioning from his current position as Representative Director, Senior Managing Executive Officer and Business Head, International Life Insurance. Takashi Iida will become Managing Executive Officer and Business Head, Asset Management, moving from his existing role as Managing Executive Officer and Business Head, Retirement, Savings and Asset Management.

Hironori Takahashi has been appointed Managing Executive Officer and Business Head, Insurance (Japan) after serving as a Managing Executive Officer. Similarly, Atsuko Yasuda and Makoto Hishida, who were previously Executive Officers, will now serve as Managing Executive Officers. Taisuke Nishimura has been elevated to Managing Executive Officer, transitioning from Executive Officer, while Yotaro Numata advances to Managing Executive Officer from his earlier position as Executive Officer and Group Chief Human Resources Officer.

Akifumi Kai will take on the position of Managing Executive Officer and Business Head, International Life Insurance, moving up from being an Executive Officer, and Webster Coates becomes Managing Executive Officer and Group Chief Compliance Officer, advancing from his prior title of Executive Officer and Group Chief Compliance Officer. Kyoko Wada, who was the Executive Officer and Group Chief Communications Officer, will now serve as Managing Executive Officer and Group Chief Communications Officer. Masakatsu Mizukami, formerly Executive Officer Status will now hold the designation of Executive Officer and and Deputy Chief General Manager, Asia Pacific, while Yukiko Sakai will continue as Executive Officer and Group Chief Sustainability Officer, and will no longer assume role of Head of Sustainability Unit.

Takehiko Eguchi, Motonobu Hoshino, Kenichi Ono, Yuichiro Abe, and Shinichiro Kaneko will transition from Executive Officer (Status) to Managing Executive Officer (Status). Alongside these changes, several officers will retire effective March 31, 2026. Mamoru Akashi, currently a Senior Managing Executive Officer, will retire and subsequently be appointed as Representative Director and President of The Dai-ichi Building Co.

Ltd. on April 1, 2026. Kohei Kai, who serves as Senior Managing Executive Officer and Business Head, Protection (Japan), will retire and then move into the role of Representative Director and Senior Managing Executive Officer of Daiichi Life Insurance Co. Ltd. effective April 1, 2026.

Hiroshi Shoji, the Managing Executive Officer and Group Chief Internal Audit Officer, will retire and become Advisor of Daiichi Frontier Life Insurance Co. Ltd. the next day, and Takashi Uehara, an Executive Officer, will retire but subsequently assume the role of Managing Executive Officer of Daiichi Life Insurance Co. Ltd. effective April 1, 2026.

The company has also announced the appointment of new officers, effective April 1, 2026. Hiroyuki Kanou will be appointed Senior Managing Executive Officer, transitioning from his current position as Representative Director and Senior Managing Executive Officer of The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, and will also serve concurrently as Representative Director and President of Daiichi Frontier Life Insurance Co. Ltd. Hiroko Ishii will take on the position of Executive Officer and Group Chief Internal Audit Officer, moving from her previous role as General Manager, Office of the Audit & Supervisory Board Member at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited.

Hiroyuki Kanou graduated from Kyushu University, Faculty of Economics, in March 1991, and in April 1991 he joined The Dai-ichi Mutual Life Insurance Company. In April 2012, he became General Manager of the Tachikawa Branch Office of The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, and in April 2015 he was appointed General Manager of the Retail Management Department. By October 2016, he had advanced to Executive Officer and General Manager of the Retail Management Department at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited.

In April 2017, he simultaneously served as Executive Officer and General Manager of the Human Resources Unit at Dai-ichi Life Holdings, Inc., as well as Executive Officer and General Manager of the Human Resources Department at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited. Two years later, in April 2019, he continued as an Executive Officer at Dai-ichi Life Holdings, Inc., and subsequently, in April 2020, he became Director and Managing Executive Officer at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited. Most recently, in April 2023, he was appointed Representative Director and Senior Managing Executive Officer.

Hiroko Ishii graduated from Kobe University, Faculty of Economics, in March 1993, and joined The Dai-ichi Mutual Life Insurance Company in April 1993. In April 2014, she became Deputy General Manager of the Credit Department at The Dai-ichi Life Insurance Company, Limited, a role she continued in October 2016 following the company?s structural shift. In April 2017, she was appointed Deputy General Manager of the Equity Investment Department, and by April 2019 she advanced to General Manager of the Responsible Investment Center.

One year later, in April 2020, she became General Manager of the Responsible Investment Department, and in April 2022 she returned to the Credit Department as General Manager. Most recently, in April 2025, she assumed the role of General Manager of the Office of the Audit & Supervisory Board Member, continuing her long-standing leadership within the organization.

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care Stocks Advance Late Afternoon
Dentsu Group Inc. Announces Management Changes, Effective from March 27, 2026
data.description:
Dentsu Group Inc. announced management changes, Effective from March 27, 2026. The following executives will step down from the Group Management Team: Hiroshi Igarashi (Director, Representative Executive Officer, President & Global CEO, dentsu), Arinobu Soga (Director, Representative Executive Officer, Executive Vice President, Global Chief Governance Officer, dentsu), and Giulio Malegori (Executive Officer, Executive Vice President, Global Chief Operating Officer, dentsu & Chairman, dentsu Americas) who will transition to become Executive Senior Advisor. Beth Ann Kaminkow's Position as of March 27, 2026 is CEO, dentsu Americas & Chief Global Client Officer .

Noritaka Omi's Position as of March 27, 2026 is Global Chief Transformation Officer, dentsu and current is Global Chief Business Operations Officer, dentsu. Jean Lin's Position as of March 27, 2026 is Global Chief Brand Officer, dentsu and current is Global President - Global Practices, dentsu. Yoshiki Ishihara's Position as of March 27, 2026 is Chief New Ventures Officer, dentsu and current is Global Chief Strategy Officer, dentsu.

Tadashi Nagae 's Position as of March 27, 2026 is Corporate Secretary & Deputy General Counsel, dentsu and current is Global Corporate Secretary & Deputy Global General Counsel, dentsu. Manus Wheeler 's Position as of March 27, 2026 is Chief of Staff, dentsu and current is Chief Brand & Culture Officer, dentsu.

data.title: GMR Airports Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
GMR Airports Limited reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was INR 39,940.3 million compared to INR 26,532.4 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 40,827.7 million compared to INR 27,482.2 million a year ago. Net income was INR 1,218.3 million compared to INR 2,667.9 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.12 compared to INR 0.25 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.12 compared to INR 0.22 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was INR 108,692.5 million compared to INR 75,509 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 111,578.5 million compared to INR 78,592 million a year ago. Net loss was INR 1,268.6 million compared to INR 1,552.6 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was INR 0.12 compared to INR 0.18 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was INR 0.12 compared to INR 0.18 a year ago.

United's stalled A350 order thrown into doubt by Rolls-Royce engine row
data.description:
CHICAGO, Feb 13 (Reuters) - United Airlines has injected new uncertainty into its long-delayed Airbus A350 order after disclosing a dispute with engine maker Rolls-Royce, the latest twist in one of the industry's longest-running jet procurement sagas.

The A350 order dates back to 2009, a year before United merged with Continental Airlines, which was a champion of Boeing and with which it had exclusive ties. The A350 deal was later restructured but has been repeatedly deferred or changed.

Now, in a regulatory filing late on Thursday, the airline said it is in a dispute with engine maker Rolls-Royce and gave investors no timetable for when the planes would arrive.

Since Britain's Rolls-Royce is the only company that makes engines for the A350, the dispute raises a question mark over whether United will be able to bring the jets into service.

In the filing, United continued to show its 45 Airbus A350 jets on its books for arrival after 2027, but offered no expected delivery timetable. A previous annual filing had pointed to expected deliveries beyond 2026.

ENGINE CONTRACT DISPUTE

The carrier said it had signed a long-term contract with Rolls-Royce in 2010 to buy engines and maintenance services, and paid the British engine maker $175 million upfront in 2017.

It claimed that in December last year, Rolls-Royce breached the terms of their contract, prompting United to demand its $175 million back, plus an additional amount. Rolls-Royce denied breaking any contract.

According to the U.S. carrier's version of events, Rolls-Royce refused to pay and argued United was at fault. Both companies are now arguing the other side owes them money, it added.

A Rolls-Royce spokesperson said the company had complied with its obligations under agreements dating back to 2010, but could not comment further due to legal proceedings.

Rolls-Royce called United a "valued customer" and said it looked forward to resolving what it called a "historic issue" and strengthening the partnership. "We're aware of the issue and are confident in our position," the spokesperson said.

THREE-WAY PRESSURE POINT

Analysts say multi-billion-dollar jet deals involve complex three-way ties between the airline, the planemaker and the engine supplier.

In a likely reference to Airbus, United said it is weighing what the dispute could mean for other parties.

Airbus declined to comment. It continues to show the stalled order on its published backlog in what industry sources describe as a routine move to safeguard any contractual rights.

A United spokesperson declined to comment beyond the filing.

United Airlines is a major buyer and has been a battleground over the years between Airbus and Boeing.

In September 2025, Chief Executive Scott Kirby told Reuters the airline expected to announce its decision on the A350 order later that year.

The airline has said a decision on the aircraft is tied to its long-term widebody replacement needs, particularly the need to eventually replace older Boeing 767s and 777s.

But the repeated deferrals have fueled speculation United could cancel the order or convert it into other Airbus aircraft, such as from the A321neo family that it has bought in large numbers.

(Reporting by Rajesh Kumar Singh and Tim Hepher in Paris; Editing by Chris Reese)

By Rajesh Kumar Singh and Tim Hepher

Sands China Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Sands China Ltd. reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was USD 7,443 million compared to USD 7,080 million a year ago. Net income was USD 896 million compared to USD 1,045 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.1108 compared to USD 0.1291 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.1108 compared to USD 0.1291 a year ago.

data.title: Microsoft, Ericsson lead global tech alliance for digital trust
FTSE 100 closes green, UK plans big missile spend
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Stock prices in London closed mostly higher on Friday, following the news that US consumer inflation slowed more than expected last month.

The US annual CPI rate stood at 2.4% on-year in January, lower than 2.7% in December and below the FXStreet-cited consensus of a softer deceleration to 2.5%. Monthly, consumer prices rose 0.2% after a 0.3% increase in December, below the consensus for another 0.3% increase.

The annual core index that strips out food and energy was up 2.5% as expected in January, slowed from 2.6%. Monthly, core goods prices were up 0.3%, also as expected, and accelerated from 0.2%.

The FTSE 100 index closed up 43.91 points, 0.4%, at 10,446.35. The FTSE 250 ended up 122.28 points, 0.5%, at 23,427.27, and the AIM all-share closed up 0.69 points, 0.1%, at 811.85.

The Cboe UK 100 was up 0.4% at 1,040.01, the Cboe UK 250 was up 0.6% at 20,757.65, and the Cboe small companies was down 0.3% at 18,614.83.

NatWest was the lowest blue-chip, down 4.1% despite reporting better-than-expected annual results.

The Edinburgh-based lender reported GBP16.64 billion in total income for 2025, up 13% on-year and ahead of analyst consensus of GBP16.53 billion. Operating pretax profit increased 24% to GBP7.71 billion, also surpassing the consensus estimate of GBP7.49 billion.

Noting that NatWest's results were "strong", Shore said: "The print reinforces NatWest as a high?teens?return on tangible equity, capital?generative UK franchise with growing tangible net asset value, benign credit trends, disciplined cost control and clear headroom for dividends and buybacks."

On AIM, EPE Special Opportunities closed 9.8% higher.

The Epic Investment Partners-managed firm announced plans to undertake up to GBP3.0 million in share buybacks, using existing cash reserves. It said the programme may exceed 25% of the average daily trading volume, due to the low liquidity of its shares in issue.

Tern was down 10%, after launching an open offer to raise up to GBP384,408 through the issue of up to 96.1 million shares at 0.40 pence each, a 20% discount to the prior closing price.

Tern says the offer was not underwritten, and that shareholders are entitled to subscribe on the basis of one new share for every seven held.

In other UK news, Britain will spend GBP400 million developing long-range missiles this year as part of "a new deal for European security", Defence Secretary John Healey has announced.

The funds will go toward replacing the Storm Shadow missile, including the Stratus "stealth" missile being developed with France and Italy, and the Deep Precision Strike system being built with Germany. Healey is expected to discuss both projects, as well as further industrial cooperation with European allies, at the Munich Security Conference, which began on Friday.

Defence stocks climbed as Melrose Industries was 3.7% higher, Rolls-Royce was up 3.6%, while BAE Systems climbed 2.2%.

In European equities on Friday, the CAC 40 in Paris closed down 0.4%, while the DAX 40 in Frankfurt was up 0.2%.

The pound was quoted at USD1.3626 at the time of the London equities close on Friday, slightly down from USD1.3628 on Thursday. The euro stood at USD1.1868, almost flat against USD1.1869. Against the yen, the dollar was trading at JPY152.88, higher compared to JPY152.56.

Stocks in New York were higher. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.3%, the S&P 500 index up 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite up marginally.

The yield on the US 10-year Treasury was quoted at 4.06%, narrowing from 4.12%. The yield on the US 30-year Treasury was quoted at 4.70%, narrowing from 4.76%.

Brent oil was quoted at USD67.48 a barrel at the time of the London equities close on Friday, down from USD68.08 late Thursday.

Gold was quoted higher at USD5,016.78 an ounce against USD4,932.33.

The biggest risers on the FTSE 100 were 3i, up 142.20p at 3,411.40p, Melrose, up 23.17p at 646.17p, Rolls-Royce, up 44.38p at 1,270.38p, Halma, up 134.00p at 3,876.00p, and Fresnillo, up 90.00p at 3,858.00p.

The biggest fallers on the FTSE 100 were NatWest, down 24.40p at 570.60p, Barclays, down 13.51p at 450.09p, Entain, down 16.87p at 578.33p, Croda, down 77.00p at 3,056.00p, and HSBC, down 26.49p at 1,240.11p.

On Monday's economic calendar, US markets are closed to mark Washington's Birthday. The eurozone and Japan release industrial production data.

On Monday's UK corporate calendar, no significant events are currently scheduled.

By Emma Curzon, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

data.title: NextEra Energy Raises Quarterly Dividend by 10%
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher


NextEra Energy's board has raised the clean-energy company's quarterly dividend by 10%, to 62.32 cents from 56.65 cents.

The new payout, equal to about $2.49 a year, represents an annual yield of around 2.63% based on the 52-week high trading price reached on Friday of $94.73.

The Juno Beach, Fla., company said the increased dividend is payable March 16 to shareholders of record Feb. 27.


Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

02-13-26 1202ET
